scenario: Jun 29-30: Iran actively blocking Hormuz reopening. Central route closed by mines. Oman/UN IMO southern route proposal abandoned after Iran attacked a Singaporean tanker using it on Jun 25. Iran FM Araghchi insists only Tehran can decide reopening. MOU (Jun 18) requires only Iran's 'best endeavours' — weak obligation. Limited northern-route traffic under Iranian control remains. France/UK naval taskforce ready to police any agreement. Brent $73.68 (Yahoo Finance Jun 30, -2.1% from Jun 25 $75.04; -27% from D93 peak). Goldman Q4 $80 Brent — contingent on Hormuz reopening timeline. Ras Laffan LNG restart not before late Aug 2026. Tail raised to 15% on ship attack escalation risk.

categories.label: Food & Agriculture
categories.supplyChainImpact: Middle East urea and DAP fertilizer exports transit Hormuz, supplying ~30% of Asia's planted-area inputs. Shipping delays of 10-14 days via Cape route raise input costs and threaten planting windows for Vietnam, Thailand, and Indonesia rice belts. Palm oil - Asia's dominant cooking oil - faces surging logistics costs as Malaysia and Indonesia divert vessels. Deal announcement reduces near-term cost pressure but mine-clearing and Qafco restart are the binding constraints on supply normalisation.

assets.name: Wheat
assets.narrative: Wheat normalising on roadmap progress (Jun 22-23 Switzerland; Brent $75.04 Jun 25, -24% from Jun 3 peak). Freight cost premium unwinding as Cape rerouting eases; mine-clearing Day 7. Goldman Q4 $80 reduces food/energy cost correlation further. Qafco force majeure lifting timeline tethered to mine-clearing (~30-day mandate from Jun 19). Source: Barchart ZWK26 (est.).

assets.name: Corn
assets.narrative: Corn normalising on crude correlation (Brent $75.04 Jun 25, -24% from peak; Goldman Q4 $80). Biofuel demand outlook softer with oil lower. Cape routing cost premium unwinding as roadmap progresses. Tail 8%. Source: CME ZCK26 (est.).

assets.name: Soybeans
assets.narrative: Soybeans normalising on crude correlation (Brent $75.04 Jun 25; Goldman Q4 $80). Bunker cost premium easing on roadmap progress and mine-clearing Day 7. Cape routing costs unwinding. Tail 8%. Source: CME ZSK26 (est.).

assets.name: Palm Oil (CPO)
assets.narrative: CPO easing on crude correlation (Brent $75.04 Jun 25, -24% from peak; Goldman Q4 $80). Indonesia B40 mandate structural floor intact. Malaysia supply route improving as mine-clearing proceeds. Roadmap agreed Jun 22-23; tanker traffic recovering. Source: Bursa Malaysia BMD (est.).

assets.name: Rice (Thai 25% FOB)
assets.narrative: Rice normalising as roadmap progress reduces food security risk premium (Brent $75.04 Jun 25; tail 8%, down from 42% D93). Qafco force majeure lifting tethered to mine-clearing Day 7; planting window pressure easing with supply resumption expected. Source: Bangkok export quotes (est.).

categories.label: Petrochemicals & Plastics
categories.supplyChainImpact: SABIC (Saudi), QAPCO (Qatar), and OCI (Oman) are among the world's largest petrochemical exporters - all ship via Hormuz. SE Asian crackers in Singapore, Thailand, and Malaysia rely on naphtha feedstock sourced from Gulf condensate. Downstream plastics, packaging, and agrochemicals face double squeeze: higher feedstock costs and logistics disruption. Deal announcement reduces near-term pressure, but physical flows through Hormuz depend on mine-clearing timeline (~6 months from signing).

assets.name: Naphtha (CFR Japan)
assets.narrative: Naphtha normalising on crude (Brent $75.04 Jun 25, -24% from peak; Goldman Q4 $80). Gulf condensate supply resumption expected post-mine clearance. Mine-clearing Day 7 of 30-day mandate. Tail 8%. Source: ICIS/Platts (est.).

assets.name: Ethylene (CFR NE Asia)
assets.narrative: Ethylene easing on naphtha/crude normalisation (Brent $75.04 Jun 25). SABIC/QAPCO shipping bottlenecks persist until mine-clearing complete (Day 7 of 30-day mandate). Tail 8%. Source: ICE/ICIS CFR NE Asia (est.).

assets.name: Methanol (CFR China)
assets.narrative: Methanol below 90d baseline on roadmap progress (Brent $75.04 Jun 25; Goldman Q4 $80). Qatar force majeure lifting tethered to Ras Laffan restart (late Aug 2026) and mine-clearing (Day 7). Sanctions waivers under deal terms easing some OFAC constraints. Source: Investing.com CFR China (est.).

categories.label: Fertilizers
categories.supplyChainImpact: The Strait of Hormuz is the critical export artery for Middle East fertilizers: Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Oman, and UAE together account for ~18% of global urea supply and ~12% of DAP. QatarEnergy (Qafco) - world's largest urea exporter - declared force majeure on Mar 2. Iran is also a significant potash and urea producer. Deal announcement reduces long-run supply risk, but Hormuz mine-clearing (~6 months from Jun 19 signing) is the binding constraint on physical export resumption.

assets.name: Urea (FOB Middle East)
assets.narrative: Urea still elevated despite roadmap progress (zscore 2.13). Qafco force majeure lifting tethered to mine-clearing Day 7 of 30-day mandate; planting window demand acute. Down from D93 $745 peak but supply cannot normalise until mine clearance + Ras Laffan restart (late Aug). Tail 8%: deal collapse spikes back toward $700+. Source: DTN, Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: DAP (FOB US Gulf)
assets.narrative: DAP easing on roadmap progress but still above baseline (zscore 1.20). Saudi Ma'aden shipping resumption pending mine-clearing (Day 7). Wet-season planting window in Philippines and Vietnam acute — demand not abating quickly. Source: Argus Fertilizers (est.).

assets.name: Potash (MOP CFR SE Asia)
assets.narrative: Potash freight premium easing on oil normalisation (Brent $75.04 Jun 25; Goldman Q4 $80). Cape rerouting cost unwinding as mine-clearing proceeds (Day 7). Palm oil plantations: input cost pressure easing further. Source: Argus Fertilizers / CRU (est.).

categories.label: Shipping & Freight
categories.supplyChainImpact: P&I club war risk coverage withdrawal (effective March 5) triggered mass tanker re-routing via Cape of Good Hope, adding 10-14 transit days and absorbing ~8% of global VLCC fleet capacity. Deal announcement Jun 14 triggers Cape premium unwinding: carriers pricing Hormuz re-opening and rerouting reversal. Physical re-entry contingent on mine-clearing (~6 months from Jun 19 signing) and P&I club re-authorisation — full shipping normalisation is a late-2026 event.

assets.name: Baltic Dry Index
assets.narrative: BDI continuing to ease as roadmap progress removes crisis premium (Brent $75.04 Jun 25, -24% from D93; Goldman Q4 $80). Cape rerouting premium unwinding as tanker traffic recovers (20+ Jun 19). Mine-clearing Day 7 constrains full reversal. Source: Baltic Exchange via Hellenic Shipping News (est.).

assets.name: VLCC AG-Asia Rate
assets.narrative: VLCC rates continuing to ease on roadmap progress (Jun 22-23). Mine-clearing Day 7 of 30-day mandate; P&I clubs re-enter progressively post-clearance. Tanker traffic: 20+ transits Jun 19. AG-Asia rates normalising toward pre-crisis 65-90 WS range. Goldman end-Jul GCC normalisation supports further rate decline. Source: Seatrade Maritime / Clarkson (est.).

assets.name: Container Freight (SCFI)
assets.narrative: SCFI declining as Asia-Europe Cape premium unwinds (roadmap Jun 22-23; mine-clearing Day 7). War risk surcharges being lifted by carriers as tanker traffic recovers. Full normalisation: 2-3 months post-mine clearance. Source: Shanghai Shipping Exchange (weekly, est.).

marketContext: Iran blocking Hormuz reopening; central route mined; southern route abandoned after ship attack (Jun 25); Iran insists sole control; France/UK taskforce ready; Brent $73.68 (Yahoo Finance Jun 30); Goldman Q4 $80; IAEA access dispute; tail 15%.
